item.title: Stocks Poised to Bounce, Pound Rises on US-Iran Deal
Renault, Thales Partner on Militarized Vehicles in Defense Push
item.description: Renault SA and Thales SA are set to cooperate on militarized vehicles, combining the French carmaker’s industrial scale with the aerospace specialist’s expertise in secure communications and defense systems.

item.title: Billionaire Gina Rinehart Made ‘Significant’ SpaceX Investment
item.description: Australia’s richest person Gina Rinehart said she made “a significant investment” in SpaceX, as her mining company laid out ambitions to work with Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite company.

item.title: Inflation Worries Keep Markets Wary Even After Iran Peace Deal
item.description: Inflation worries kept investors cautious even as bonds and stocks rallied on Monday after a US-Iran interim deal eased nerves, with market participants warning that the conflict’s economic fallout remains unresolved.

item.title: London Gold Market Weighs Earlier Auction to Suit Asian Traders
item.description: The London Bullion Market Association is considering moving its morning gold auction to an earlier time to accommodate traders in Asia.

item.title: Hormuz Trade Will Take Months to Return to Normal, Analysts Say
item.description: Oil markets have reacted with enthusiasm to an interim agreement between US and Iran which should reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil and gas flows — but that return to normality could be months away, analysts warn.

item.title: What the US-Iran Peace Deal Means for the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: No region of the world produces more oil and gas than the countries straddling the Persian Gulf. Most of this energy can only be exported aboard tankers that cross the Strait of Hormuz — a waterway that’s effectively been closed for more than three months since the US and Israel started a war against Iran in late February.

item.title: South African Firms Face Pressure Due to Anti-Immigrant Protests
item.description: South African companies with operations across Africa are facing mounting pressure as anti-immigrant protests in the continent’s largest economy trigger diplomatic tensions and calls for action against their businesses.

item.title: Philippines’ Marcos Calls for Special Congress Session Amid Deadlock
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. called Congress to a special session on June 17 to pass priority legislative measures, hoping lawmakers will set aside political distractions.

item.title: Hormuz Halt Forces Middle East Trade Into Huge Rewiring
item.description: In the wake of the 12-day US-Israel and Iran war a year ago, Siemens Energy AG sent a person driving from Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea port of Jeddah across the Arabian peninsula to the industrial hub of Dammam to study an alternative route in case the crucial Strait of Hormuz was shut down.

item.title: Hong Kong’s Leader Dodges Questions on Potential Second Term
item.description: Hong Kong Chief Executive John Lee remained non-committal about seeking a second term, deflecting questions regarding his political future ahead of the city’s leadership selection next year.

item.title: Anthropic Block Marks US Reversal, Warning to Silicon Valley
item.description: The extraordinary move by the US to bar foreign access to Anthropic PBC’s best AI models underscores the Trump administration’s newfound willingness to exert control over a pivotal industry. It also reminds Silicon Valley that it’s working with an imperfectly understood technology with uncertain impact.

item.title: Cement, Textile Stocks Biggest Winners From Pakistan Budget
item.description: The government’s move to lower taxes on certain sectors including cement and textiles is likely to support related shares as Pakistan looks to balance growth and compliance with its promises to the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: Swiss Pragmatism Holds as Disputed Population Cap Rejected
item.description: Swiss voters opted to play it safe on Sunday, rejecting an unprecedented proposal to cap the country’s population at 10 million people as economic risks overcame elevated concerns about immigration.

item.title: Macron’s G7 Legacy Hangs on Fickle AI Funding and Data Centers
item.description: With less than a year left in office, Emmanuel Macron wants to be remembered as the French president who put Europe back in the technology race.

item.title: Warsh Is Inheriting a Rates-Mountain Breakdown
item.description: We’re going from a steady descent on interest rates to a potentially wild ride.

item.title: More Bank of England Officials Set to Back Hikes as Iran War Frays Unity
item.description: The Iran war has produced an unusual sight on the Bank of England’s rate-setting panel: unity. Economists don’t expect it to last.

item.title: Failed SpaceX IPO Allocation Prompts Korea to Widen Mirae Review
item.description: South Korea’s financial watchdog has expanded an inspection of Mirae Asset Securities Co. to examine the brokerage’s failure to secure an allocation of SpaceX shares in the company’s initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: 新SiriはアップルAI危機を救えるか、実機を試して見えた7つの改善例－Power On
item.description: アップルの新Siriは2年遅れで登場したものの、実用性が十分な水準に達し、AI戦略立て直しの足掛かりとなりつつある。

item.title: US-Iran Peace Deal May Keep Rally in India Stocks, Rupee Going
item.description: The rupee has emerged as Asia’s best performer over the past month.

item.title: Top Banks Rush to Fill Chief AI Roles as Talent Jumps to Rivals
item.description: Banks from Sydney to London are racing to fill the senior role of chief AI officer, a job that barely existed a year ago. Those in the position, however, say it might not be around for long.

item.title: Shipowners Seek Clarity on Hormuz Deal as 600 Vessels Eye Exit
item.description: A deal that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz within days has been met with caution among shipowners and traders, with many saying they would wait for more details in order to assess whether safe transits are possible after months of false starts.

item.title: Copper Pops Higher as US-Iran Agreement Puts Bulls in Charge
item.description: Copper rose as an interim peace agreement between the US and Iran eased fears over global economic growth and stoked optimism for metals demand.

item.title: Activist Palliser Urges Taiwan Firm WUS to Privatize, Lift Value
item.description: Activist hedge fund Palliser Capital is urging Taiwanese company WUS Printed Circuit Co. to enhance shareholder value, including through a potential privatization, according to documents viewed by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Zhipu Shares Surge 48% After JPMorgan Picks Company as AI Winner
item.description: Shares of Chinese AI model maker Zhipu surged after JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised the stock’s price target and picked it as a winner against close rival MiniMax.

item.title: Singapore to Launch Gold Clearing With JPMorgan, Other Banks
item.description: Singapore plans to launch a gold-clearing system this year, with banks including JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Deutsche Bank AG set to participate in the city-state’s push to become a hub in the global bullion market.

item.title: Korea’s JoongAng Seeks Rehabilitation After Broadcaster Default
item.description: South Korean media giant JoongAng Group has filed for court rehabilitation after its broadcaster defaulted on a loan, with the company hit by financial strain linked to its purchase of expensive sports broadcasting rights acquired at the peak of the media market.

item.title: Crop Prices Drop as Hormuz Reopening Would Ease Farm Input Shock
item.description: Grain futures declined in Chicago as a potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could improve access to vital crop inputs, helping to ease the threats to food inflation brought on by the months-long war.

item.title: Treasuries Rally as Traders Trim Fed Hike Bets After Iran Deal
item.description: Treasuries advanced across the curve as investors dialed back expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes following news of a deal to halt the Iran war.

item.title: IFM Investors Raises Price in Atlas Arteria Takeover Bid
item.description: IFM Investors has increased its bid and removed certain conditions in its best and final offer for Australian-listed toll road operator Atlas Arteria Ltd.
